data.narrative:
Skyroot Aerospace is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $95M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five same-sector, same-stage private peers (Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace, and Loft Orbital) and five public-market anchors (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $95M total raised as a proxy baseline with a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $162M. Peer-median analysis across the five comparable private companies yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median: 42.9x across six public tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data. These two methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $137M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median implied value of $656M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Skyroot's profile aligns with lower-stage peer benchmarks or with more mature Series C cohort valuations. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, launch cadence, and customer concentration—factors not quantified in available data.
